title: Examine SweRV WIDTH rule warnings for correctness etc
body:
Hi,

as response to 
https://github.com/chipsalliance/Cores-SweRV-EL2/pull/

we had to block WIDTH and UNOPTFLAT warnings as we used verilator 4.020 for cores release.
Seems majority of warnings are not helpful or incorrect. 

What is the current verilator "production" version?

Was these warnings detection improved?

Here are examples for EL2

```
%Warning-WIDTH: ... design/elx2_pic_ctrl.sv:412: Operator LT expects 32 bits on the RHS, but RHS's SEL generates 9 bits.
                                                                                                          : ... In instance tb_top.rvtop.swerv.pic_ctrl_inst
         for (int i=0; i<pt.PIC_TOTAL_INT_PLUS1; i++) begin

```

here we use sized parameters - the tool complains about size difference between parameter constant and integer . I would expect warnings/errors only in case where the constant does not fit a variable or   unsigned variable  gets/compared with negative constant.

```
Warning-WIDTH: ... estbench/tb_top.sv:348: Operator ASSIGNDLY expects 1 bits on the Assign RHS, but Assign RHS's REPLICATE generates 2 bits.
                                                                                                      : ... In instance tb_top
        wb_valid  <= {wb_valid[1],rvtop.swerv.dec.dec_i0_wen_r};

```

wb_valid is array of two bits : logic wb_valid[2]
